name: The Kid Who Took Over Twitter

video.title: The Kid Who Took Over Twitter
video.global_style_suffix: 3d mannequin documentary, faceless matte humanoid mannequin figures with no facial features, stylized 3d reconstruction environment, dark low-key lighting, deep shadows, one red rim-light accent, desaturated cinematic documentary grade, filmic grain
video.global_negative_prompt: cartoon, anime, illustration, detailed human faces, facial features, oversaturated, neon colors, bright cheerful lighting, watermark, readable text, gibberish letters, low quality, deformed

direction_card.hook_style: open mid-moment — someone is already doing something at a specific time of night
direction_card.ending: freeze on the question the story never answered

voice.speaker: Aiden
voice.instruct: Calm, precise male documentary narrator, mid-low register, measured newsreader trust. Present tense storytelling with real pauses: a beat after each reveal, a longer beat before chapter turns. Slightly tighter pace during escalation, settling quiet and thoughtful at the end with falling cadence. Dry humor read completely straight. Quoted dialogue gets a subtle cadence shift, never a character voice.

settings.music_vibe: somber investigative ambient, quiet pulse, restrained tension, minimal piano and low strings

scenes.narration: A seventeen year old is about to take over the most powerful accounts on the internet.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, miniature diorama model of a single suburban Florida house at night glowing from one upstairs window, tiny palm trees, tabletop scale model on black, wide diorama shot, one red spotlight

scenes.narration: At 20:17 UTC, Elon Musk's Twitter account posts a message.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, a faceless mannequin in a plain black t-shirt standing in a rocket hangar beside a tall white rocket fuselage, holding a phone loosely at his side, wide reenactment shot, cold industrial light, red hazard lamp

Consistent art style throughout, on-model, no style change, no repainting. Subtle deliberate motion

scenes.narration: It promises to double any bitcoin sent to an address below.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, macro shot of a single physical bitcoin coin resting on a cracked phone screen in the dark, extreme close-up, shallow focus, red rim light

video_meta.prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, faceless matte humanoid mannequin figures with no facial features, stylized 3d reconstruction environment, dark low-key lighting, deep shadows, one red rim-light accent, desaturated cinematic documentary grade, filmic grain. 3d mannequin documentary, dark map visualization, glowing red transaction lines spreading across a black world map, top-down — clearly showing promises double any bitcoin sent address below. 3d mannequin documentary, dark map visualization, glowing red transaction lines spreading across a black world map, top-down — clearly showing promises double any bitcoin sent address below. subtle ambient motion, gentle idle movement and breathing, slow drifting camera, the scene stays composed and on-model. scenes.narration: Then Barack Obama's account posts it. Then Joe Biden's. Then Jeff Bezos and Bill Gates.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, four faceless mannequins standing at separate lecterns in a dark hall: one tall in a navy suit, one in aviator sunglasses, one bald in a quilted puffer vest, one in a v-neck sweater with round glasses, wide group reenactment, single hard key light, red back glow

scenes.narration: Apple posts it. Uber posts it.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, split scene: a minimalist glass storefront with a brushed aluminium laptop on a pale wood table, and a black sedan with a lit windshield rideshare sign idling on a wet night street, wide two-part composition, dark, red tail-light accent

scenes.narration: Within minutes, the scam collects more than one hundred thousand dollars.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, a heap of physical bitcoin coins tumbling into a dark pile on a steel table, mid-motion, close reenactment shot, single overhead lamp, deep shadow, red rim

scenes.narration: Twitter's answer is drastic. It silences almost every verified account on the platform.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, wide shot of an empty open-plan tech office at night, rows of dark monitors all switched to a flat blue glow, chairs pushed back, nobody around, cold blue light, one red exit sign glow
scenes.camera: slow aerial push-in toward, cinematic drone shot

scenes.narration: For a few hours, the loudest voices on Earth cannot speak.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, a dark empty press briefing room, rows of microphones on an abandoned podium, single spotlight, nobody around, wide establisher, deep shadows

scenes.narration: Here is how a teenager did it.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, security-camera framing of a faceless mannequin teenager silhouetted in a bedroom doorway, grainy CCTV look, timestamp-free, high angle surveillance shot, green-grey night vision tint, red recording glow

scenes.narration: His name is Graham Ivan Clark. He is a high school student in Hillsborough County.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, a faceless mannequin teenager in a school hallway wearing a backpack, lockers receding behind him, medium reenactment, flat fluorescent light, one red locker door

scenes.narration: He is not a prodigy in the movie sense. He is patient, and he is good on the phone.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, close reenactment of a faceless mannequin leaning forward mid-phone-call, phone pressed to where an ear would be, free hand raised mid-gesture, scattered notes on the desk, dark room, red rim light

scenes.narration: A year earlier, at sixteen, he had already stolen a hundred and sixty four bitcoin from a Seattle investor.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, macro shot of a SIM card being lifted from a phone tray by faceless mannequin fingers, extreme close-up, shallow focus, black background, single red highlight
scenes.camera: slow push-in on, shallow focus, deliberate zoom

scenes.narration: He did it with a SIM swap. He convinced a phone carrier that he was someone else.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, a faceless mannequin in a call-centre headset seated at a carrier service desk, gesturing at a screen, a second mannequin customer waiting, medium two-shot reenactment, cold office light, red monitor glow

scenes.narration: That is the whole trick, in every story that follows. He does not break the machine. He talks to the people who run it.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, miniature diorama of a telephone exchange building sliced open like a doll's house, tiny mannequins inside at switchboards, tabletop model on black, wide diorama, red wiring glow

scenes.narration: The Twitter attack begins on the 15th of July with phone calls.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, close on a black desk phone mid-ring, handset lifting off the cradle, dark office, hard single light, deep shadow, red accent

scenes.narration: Clark and two others scrape LinkedIn for Twitter employees who might hold administrative access.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, a wall of employee profile photographs pinned on dark corkboard, blank faceless portraits, red string connecting three of them, investigator's board, medium shot, low-key light
scenes.camera: slow lateral pan across, steady tracking

scenes.narration: Then they call them, one at a time, pretending to be Twitter's own IT help desk.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, split reenactment: a faceless mannequin teenager on a phone in a dim bedroom on the left, a faceless mannequin office worker picking up a desk phone in a bright cubicle on the right, wide split composition, cold office light versus red bedroom glow

scenes.narration: There is a problem with the internal VPN, the caller says. He sounds bored. He sounds like a colleague.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, close reenactment of the office mannequin holding a phone against its shoulder while typing, bored posture, cubicle walls, medium close-up, flat fluorescent light

scenes.narration: The employee is walked to a login page that looks exactly like Twitter's own.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, macro of a laptop screen showing a plain blue login form with two empty input boxes, no letters, reflection of a faceless mannequin face on the glass, extreme close-up, shallow focus, blue glow
scenes.camera: slow push-in on, shallow focus, deliberate zoom

scenes.narration: They type in their credentials. They read out the two factor code.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, extreme close-up of faceless mannequin fingers typing on a mechanical keyboard, one hand holding a phone displaying a six-digit code panel, no readable characters, macro, dark, red rim light

scenes.narration: On the other end of the line, in Tampa, the code is used within seconds.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, the teenage mannequin lunging toward his monitor in the dark bedroom, both hands on the keyboard, tense forward posture, medium action reenactment, red glow across his shoulders

scenes.narration: That gives them the one thing that matters. The internal tool that Twitter's own staff use to control any account.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, a plain administrative control panel glowing on a wide monitor, rows of blank toggle switches and empty fields, no letters, faceless mannequin reflected in the glass, medium shot, blue-white screen light

scenes.narration: They start with short usernames. The kind collectors pay for. Then they aim higher.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, a row of faceless mannequin heads on a dark shelf like collectible busts, price tags blank, the last one lit red, medium detail shot, gallery lighting, deep shadow
scenes.camera: slow lateral pan across, steady tracking

scenes.narration: By 21:45 UTC, Twitter admits it has lost control of its own platform.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, wide shot of an empty glass-walled tech headquarters at night seen from outside, one floor still lit, city reflections, drone establisher, cold blue, red aircraft warning light
scenes.camera: slow aerial push-in toward, cinematic drone shot

scenes.narration: Coinbase blocks over a thousand transactions, about two hundred and eighty thousand dollars that never reaches the scam.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, dark top-down map visualization, glowing red money-flow lines racing across a black continent then hitting a wall of blocking nodes and stopping, data map shot

scenes.narration: The scam itself is almost small. About a hundred and ten thousand dollars in bitcoin.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, a single small stack of bitcoin coins alone on a vast empty dark table, wide negative space, one hard overhead light, deep shadow

scenes.narration: The damage is not the money. It is the proof that three people with a telephone could seize the megaphone of the planet.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, a faceless mannequin holding an old megaphone standing on a tiny stage before a vast dark empty auditorium of thousands of blank seats, wide dramatic reenactment, single beam, red haze

scenes.narration: And Clark leaves a trail. He always did.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, surveillance-camera framing of a faceless mannequin walking away down a suburban street at night, seen from a doorbell camera, grainy CCTV look, high angle, night vision tint

scenes.narration: Investigators follow the bitcoin, and they follow the usernames he used on hacking forums.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, a dark investigator's wall: printed transaction slips and forum screenshots pinned in a chain, red string linking them, blank paper with no letters, medium detail shot, desk lamp
scenes.camera: slow lateral pan across, steady tracking

scenes.narration: Some of those accounts were tied to an email address.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, macro of an old envelope and a blank email printout on a metal desk under a magnifier, extreme close-up, shallow focus, cold light, red pen mark

scenes.narration: The email was tied to a driver's license he had once uploaded to verify himself on a crypto exchange.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, macro of a blank plastic identification card lying face up on a dark scanner bed, glass glowing, no readable text, extreme close-up, single red scanner line crossing it

scenes.narration: The most careful social engineer in the story had verified his own identity, in writing, to a company that keeps records.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, a faceless mannequin sitting alone at a bare metal table in a dark interrogation room, hands folded, one empty chair opposite, wide reenactment, single overhead lamp, deep shadow

scenes.narration: On the 31st of July, sixteen days after the attack, police arrest him in Tampa.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, police cruisers with strobing light bars parked across a suburban driveway at night, faceless mannequin officers in duty vests moving toward the door, wide action reenactment, red and blue light washing the house

scenes.narration: He is seventeen. Florida charges him as an adult, with thirty felony counts.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, an empty courtroom at night, dark wood benches, a single lamp over the judge's bench, one faceless mannequin standing small in the dock, wide establisher, deep shadows

scenes.narration: In March 2021, he takes a plea deal. Three years in prison, three years of probation, under Florida's youthful offender law.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, miniature diorama of a prison block sliced open like a model, tiny corridors and cells, one cell lit red, tabletop scale model on black, wide diorama shot
scenes.camera: slow aerial push-in toward, cinematic drone shot

scenes.narration: Two others were charged in the United States. We are not going to pretend the full picture is settled. Parts of it are still disputed.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, two faceless mannequins standing far apart in a vast dark empty space, a third silhouette half-dissolving into shadow, wide composition, dim rim light

scenes.narration: Twitter changed its internal tools afterwards. It says access is tighter now.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, an empty glass office lobby at night behind security barriers, badge readers glowing, nobody around, wide establisher, cold blue light, red reader glow

scenes.narration: But the method he used is not a software flaw. It is a person, picking up a phone, wanting to be helpful.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, close on a plain desk phone under a single hard light in an empty dark room, the handset lifted slightly off its cradle, nobody around, macro detail, red accent

scenes.narration: There is no patch for that.
scenes.image_prompt: 3d mannequin documentary, wide shot of an empty dark room with one monitor still glowing and an empty chair pushed back from the desk, nobody around, deep shadow, red rim light

characters.name: The Hacker
characters.description: faceless matte grey humanoid mannequin, no facial features, slim, teenage build, plain form, lit by monitor glow with a red rim light
characters.palette: matte grey, monitor blue, blood red accent

research.summary: On 15 July 2020, 17-year-old Graham Ivan Clark and two others phone-phished Twitter employees, seized the internal admin tool, and hijacked about 130 high-profile accounts to run a bitcoin doubling scam. Twitter silenced verified accounts for hours. Clark was arrested 31 July 2020 and pleaded guilty in March 2021.
facts: Attack ran 15 July 2020, roughly 20:00-22:00 UTC; Musk's account posted at 20:17 UTC.
About 130 accounts targeted; 45 actually used to post the scam.
Scam wallets took roughly 110,000 to 118,000 USD in bitcoin.
Coinbase blocked over 1,000 transactions worth about 280,000 USD.
Twitter acknowledged the breach by 21:45 UTC and disabled tweeting for verified accounts.
Method was phone spear-phishing: LinkedIn scraping, fake VPN portal, 2FA codes read aloud.
Graham Ivan Clark, 17, of Hillsborough County, Florida, arrested 31 July 2020; 30 felony counts.
In 2019, aged 16, Clark stole 164 bitcoin from a Seattle investor via a SIM swap.
March 2021 plea: three years prison plus three years probation (Youthful Offender Act).
Co-defendants Mason Sheppard (19) and Nima Fazeli (22) were charged federally.
sources: U.S. Department of Justice charging announcement, 31 July 2020
